title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins signed Max Lundgren, a 23-year-old goalie from Merrimack College, to a one-year entry-level contract worth $952,500.
Lundgren posted a 21-16-2 record with a 2.55 GAA and a .920 save percentage in 39 games for Merrimack last season.
Lundgren led Merrimack to its first Hockey East title and earned tournament MVP honors before joining the Bruins.

title: 2 people from Mass. killed when helicopter crashes in Hawaii
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Margaret Rimmler, 65, and Patrick Haskell, 59, from Massachusetts, died in a helicopter crash off Kauai, Hawaii.
The crash occurred near Kalalau Beach, involving a helicopter operated by Airborne Aviation with five people aboard.
Two survivors were rescued and evacuated to a medical center in Lihue, Hawaii following the crash.

title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly on March 28, 2026, accompanied by bassist John Clayton.
Krall's 85-minute concert featured jazz standards and her own work, showcasing her piano and vocal talents.
The setlist included classics like 'Almost Like Being In Love' and an encore of 'P.S. I Love You.'

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelsea teen Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after allegedly chasing and stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez, 25.
Incident occurred March 19 near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue; victim Lopez died in the hospital from multiple wounds.
Castellanos Cerrato pleaded not guilty, held without bail; court date set for April 21; Chelsea police seek information from public.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Greater Boston's population growth dropped to 11,991 in 2025, the slowest rate since the pandemic started.
Massachusetts saw a statewide population growth of 15,524 in 2025, the smallest increase since 2020.
Counties like Suffolk, Barnstable, and Hampshire experienced marginal population decreases, despite previous years' growth.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod researchers launched a GoFundMe to support North Atlantic right whale conservation, raising $630 towards a $15,000 goal.
The Marineland Right Whale Project will use funds for aerial surveys and equipment like drones and high-resolution cameras.
North Atlantic right whales face threats from vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements, critically reducing their population.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss was traded to the Patriots from the Raiders in 2007, setting an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in one season.
Wes Welker was acquired from the Dolphins in 2007, becoming a standout slot receiver but not winning a Super Bowl with the team.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, showing potential as a top receiver but facing setbacks due to off-field issues.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning of the Red Sox's 6-5 loss to the Reds.
Trevor Story's strike three on a check swing upset Boston, as umpire C.B. Bucknor declined to appeal.
Bucknor missed several calls; all five of the Reds' challenges were upheld, while Boston lost both challenges by the third inning.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire C.B. Bucknor's strike three call, leading to Red Sox manager Alex Cora's ejection.
The controversial check swing call occurred with Red Sox trailing 5-4 and two runners on base.
Despite the incident, Red Sox tied the game but lost 6-5 to fall to a 1-1 season record.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals in 16 games since the Bruins returned from the Olympic break.
Zacha missed the Olympics due to a concussion but has focused on improving his game for the Bruins.
Slotted as the second-line center, Zacha has reached a career-high of 26 goals this season with the Bruins.
